However, I can suggest a similar plant called Erythroxylum coca (Coca plant) and Erythroxylum novogalicanum, but I found more information on Erythroxylum coca.

Erythroxylum coca, also known as Coca plant (family Erythroxylaceae), is a flowering plant native to South America.

The Coca plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, which include reducing altitude sickness, improving mental clarity, and alleviating fatigue. However, its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and addiction risks.

The bioactive constituents of the Coca plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as cocaine, which is a potent stimulant, and other compounds like cinnamidine and erythroxylamine.

The most common herbal preparations made with the Coca plant are traditional tea infusions, known as mate in some countries and coca tea in others, and chewable coca leaves, often used for medicinal and cultural purposes.
